labs.initialMonitoring: Monitor for acute drop in eGFR
labs.actionRequired: Monitor eGFR for acute decline; discontinue if drops significantly
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for acute eGFR decline. Drop ≥30% was uncommon but associated with modestly increased risks of kidney adverse events.
Monitor albuminuria status. CREDENCE trial included patients with ACR 300-5000 mg/g. Canagliflozin reduced progression of albuminuria.

labs.actionRequired: Monitor for doubling of creatinine as indicator of kidney disease progression
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for doubling of serum creatinine as endpoint for kidney failure risk assessment

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: FDA-approved eGFR ≥30 ml/min per 1.73 m². Not recommended at 300 mg dose for CKD patients. Use 100 mg dose only in CKD.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: The CREDENCE trial enrolled patients with T2D and CKD, defined by an eGFR of 30-90 ml/min per 1.73 m2 with albuminuria (ACR of 300-5000 mg/g). In CREDENCE, a drop in eGFR ≥30% was uncommon (4% of participants assigned to canagliflozin) but was associated with modestly increased risks of kidney adverse events
